[initprompt] workstream-2-claim-a-planning

Planning task. Read ui-audit/02-comparison.md and ui-audit/04-recommendations.md, focusing on entries that pertain to the application shell layer: the top bar, left navigation, routing, theming, and the token registry in index.css. Also read ui-audit/05-claim-c-deferral.md to confirm exclusions.

Goal: produce ui-audit/06-claim-a-plan.md as a work plan for converging the app shell layer. The plan will be executed in subsequent prompts; this prompt only plans.

Section one, scope. Enumerate every concrete change required to converge Claim A. Group changes into two categories: (a) token registry gaps meaning tokens referenced in code but not defined in index.css, or tokens that should be defined to support recommended convergence; (b) shell drift meaning places where Console, Designer, or Settings deviate from the app-shell pattern (sidebar widths, nav active-state indicators, top-bar styling). Cite the specific row in 02-comparison.md or recommendations section in 04-recommendations.md for each change.

Section two, multi-module implications. For each change, note whether it affects only one module, multiple modules, or the shell layer itself which affects all modules. This matters because the user plans to rebuild eight additional modules around the converged Console/Designer foundation, so shell-layer decisions get inherited by eleven modules total. Flag any change where the audit's recommendation has implications that should be reviewed by the user before executing rather than decided unilaterally by the model.

Section three, sequencing. Order the changes so that token registry work happens before shell drift work, because the drift fixes will likely reference tokens that need to exist first. Within token work, order so independent gaps go first and dependent ones go second. Note any change that could not be done as a small isolated PR and requires coordination.

Section four, definition of done for Claim A. State concrete completion criteria. Examples: every token referenced in shell components is defined in index.css; sidebar widths are consistent across all three modules with a documented value; nav active-state indicators render consistently across all three modules; the comparison file's Claim A rows are marked complete with references to the PRs or commits that landed them.

Section five, implications for future work. Per the user's longer-term plan, record two things. First, implications for the Claim C revisit (workstream 5) meaning any shell decision that may affect how the canvas seam is eventually unified. Second, implications for the eight-module rebuild meaning any shell convention being established now that the eight rebuilt modules should follow.

Do not start implementation. Do not modify any other audit files. This is planning only.

When done print the file path and the count of changes in each scope category and stop.

- audit: Verify a spec unit's implementation against its authoritative spec. Reads the manifest, reads spec files, walks target code, catalogs every gap, and writes task files for each finding.
- spec: Read the implementation spec for a given I/O module. Usage: /spec <module>. Modules: console, process, designer, graphics, display-elements, shapes, opc
- design-qa: Inventory all implementations of a cross-cutting feature across the codebase, surface inconsistencies, ask targeted design questions, and write the answers as an authoritative decision file. Run this BEFORE /audit on any feature marked ⚠️ NOT SPECCED in the manifest.
- spec-index: Print a quick-reference index of every audit unit — unit ID, canonical name, wave, and current task status. Use this to look up the correct unit ID or module name before running spec-scout or feature-agent.
- agent-guide: Print a reference guide for all custom agents in this project — what each one does, when to use it, how to launch it, and usage examples.
- wrapup: Close the current work unit (summary + review + doc updates)
- review: Run a verification review of work done since the last [initprompt]
- deepreview: Run a deep verification review including full file contents
- summarize: Generate an LLM-based summary of the current work unit
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

13	| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.css:19–295` (138 tokens); hardcoded values in `AlarmListPane.tsx`, `PointTablePane.tsx`, `TrendPane.tsx`, `index.tsx` | `index.css:19–219`; `shared/theme/tokens.ts` (JS mirror); hardcoded overrides in `DesignerToolbar`, `DesignerTabBar`, `DesignerStatusBar`, `DesignerGraphicsList` | `index.css:17–219`; `settingsStyles.ts:1–71` (subset of token aliases); hardcoded overrides in `Users.tsx`, `Roles.tsx`, `Groups.tsx`, `OpcSources.tsx`, `BulkUpdate.tsx` | `index.css` is the canonical token registry for all three | — |
14	| **Visual properties actually applied** | ~20 token references; hardcoded hex for alarm priority/quality badge colors, TrendPane series palette, published dot | ~15 token references; 9 hardcoded color values in shell components; 2 undefined tokens (`--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`) | ~15 token references including `--io-border-subtle`, `--io-success`, `--io-status-fg`; undefined `--io-text-on-accent` in `btnPrimary`; hex-alpha badge concatenation bug in OpcSources | All three use `var(--io-accent)`, `var(--io-border)`, `var(--io-text-primary)`, `var(--io-text-muted)`, `var(--io-surface)` family, `var(--io-success)`/`--io-warning`/`--io-danger` | — |
15	| **Deviations from app shell** | `var(--io-bg)` and `var(--io-text)` referenced but not defined in `index.css`; alarm priority badge colors hardcoded instead of using `--io-alarm-*` tokens; published dot `#10b981` ≠ `var(--io-success)` | `var(--io-surface-hover)` and `var(--io-font-sans)` undefined; 9 hardcoded values bypass tokens; `#09090b` hardcoded for active IconBtn text instead of `var(--io-accent-foreground)` | `--io-text-on-accent` undefined in `btnPrimary` across ~15 files; hex-alpha CSS var concatenation bug (`${color}20`) renders OpcSources StatusBadge backgrounds as invalid; hardcoded modal overlay/shadow values in Users/Roles/Groups | All three reference at least one undefined token; all three have hardcoded hex colors that bypass the token system | Each module references a different set of undefined tokens |
16	| **Notes** | Console quality badge colors (`#10B981`, `#F59E0B`, `#EF4444`) have no corresponding tokens; hardcoded is the only option for that sub-type | `DesignerGraphicsList` `SCOPE_COLORS`/`MODE_COLORS` are mostly hardcoded rgba; two of six entries use registered tokens: `SCOPE_COLORS.console.text = "var(--io-accent)"` and `MODE_COLORS.report.text = "var(--io-text-secondary)"` (DesignerGraphicsList.tsx:48,55) | OpcSources StatusBadge `${color}20` concatenation bug is a functional regression: backgrounds render as transparent in all browsers | — | — |

No color properties, no CSS custom properties, no hardcoded hex. |

201	
202	## List 1 — Elements already consistent across all three modules
203	
204	1. **Token registry authority.** All three modules draw colors exclusively from the `index.css` CSS custom property registry. No module owns or overrides token definitions.
205	2. **Typography implementation pattern.** All three use raw pixel integer literals in inline styles. None reference the 16 typography scale tokens (`--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`) defined in `index.css`.
206	3. **Shared ContextMenu component.** All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` as the sole or primary right-click context menu implementation. Visual properties for context menus are governed by the shared component.
207	4. **Primary button background.** All three use `var(--io-accent)` as the background of primary action buttons.
208	5. **Secondary button border.** All three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` as the border for secondary/outline button variants.
209	6. **`--io-btn-*` tokens unused.** All three modules ignore the six `--io-btn-bg`, `--io-btn-hover`, `--io-btn-active`, `--io-btn-text`, `--io-btn-secondary-bg`, `--io-btn-secondary-border` tokens defined in `index.css`.
210	7. **`--io-input-*` tokens largely unused.** Console and Settings ignore all five `--io-input-*` tokens; Designer's `ShapePointSelector` uses `--io-input-bg` (index.css:131) and `--io-input-border` (index.css:132). Three of the five tokens remain unused across all three modules.
211	8. **Focus ring suppression.** All three suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) on form inputs without applying a CSS replacement using `--io-focus-ring` or `--io-input-focus-border`.
212	9. **Hover via DOM mutation.** All three use `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.*`) for at least some interactive hover states rather than CSS `:hover` rules or React state.
213	10. **Connection state dot pattern.** All three represent connection state (WS connection, OPC source state) with 6–8px circular dots using `var(--io-success)` for connected and `var(--io-danger)` for error.
214	11. **`--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` unused.** `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are ignored by all three modules; `--io-modal-backdrop` IS defined (index.css:139) and IS used in ≥6 Settings files. Console and Designer do not use it.
215	12. **Hardcoded primary button text.** All three hardcode the text color on accent-background buttons (`#fff`, `#09090b`, or `var(--io-text-on-accent)` which is undefined) instead of using `var(--io-accent-foreground)`.
216	
217	---
218	
219	## List 2 — Elements that are inconsistent across modules
220	
221	1. **Side panel background token.** Console and Settings both use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` for the panel background; Designer's left palette uses `var(--io-surface)` — one tier lighter.
222	2. **Modal backdrop color.** Console uses hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`; Designer uses the same range hardcoded; Settings uses five distinct specifications including one undefined token (`--io-overlay`) and one defined token (`--io-modal-backdrop`) with no consistent value among the three.
223	3. **Form input background token.** Settings canonical (`settingsStyles`) uses `var(--io-surface-sunken)`; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel` uses `var(--io-surface)`; Console uses `var(--io-surface-secondary)` or `var(--io-surface-elevated)` inconsistently — three different surface tiers across modules for the same element.
224	4. **Section / field label typography convention.** Console palette section labels are 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; Designer `FieldLabel` is 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em; Settings `labelStyle` is 12px/500 with no uppercase — same UI concept (label above a form field or palette section) implemented with three different size, weight, and transform combinations.
225	5. **StatusBadge implementation.** Console uses hardcoded hex-rgba arithmetic; Designer uses fully hardcoded colors for READ-ONLY badge; Settings has five incompatible implementations (one broken, one using `color-mix()`, one using token pairs, one dot-only, one dot+text).
226	6. **Modal accessibility (ARIA).** Console: three inline modals lack `role="dialog"`; Designer: only `RecognitionWizard` has ARIA; Settings: `RestorePreviewModal`, all `Import` dialogs, `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal` have no ARIA — each module has a different gap profile.
227	7. **Undefined tokens referenced.** All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones: Console references `--io-bg` and `--io-text`; Designer references `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-accent-muted`; Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, and `--io-accent-rgb` (genuinely undefined). Tokens previously listed for Settings that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (index.css:139), `--io-info` (index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (index.css:29).
228	8. **Button hover states.** Designer's `IconBtn` has a 0.1s transition to `var(--io-surface-elevated)` on hover; Console has no hover state on any toolbar or modal button; Settings has no hover or focus styles on any button variant.
229	9. **Toolbar structure.** Console has one 48px header bar; Designer has four stacked bars totaling 144px of chrome (36+44+36+28); Settings has no toolbar.
230	10. **Menu pattern consistency.** Settings uses only the shared `ContextMenu` component; Console uses it alongside one hand-rolled dropdown; Designer uses it alongside two custom dropdown implementations with different positioning strategies and z-index values.
231	11. **Modal z-index values.** Console: 9999/1001/4000; Designer: 1000–3000 (six distinct values); Settings: multiple with no registry — all uncoordinated with the `--io-z-modal: 300` token.
232	12. **Semantic heading elements.** Console uses no semantic heading elements anywhere; Designer uses `<h1>`/`<h2>`/`<h3>` in `SymbolLibrary` and `DesignerGraphicsList` but `<div>` elsewhere; Settings uses `<h2>` in `SettingsPageLayout` — no consistent policy across or within modules.
233	
234	---
235	
236	## List 3 — Elements where one module is the best version others could converge on
237	
238	1. **Form input centralization — Settings via `settingsStyles.ts`.** `settingsStyles.ts` defines a single named `inputStyle` object that propagates automatically to all pages that import it. Console has no equivalent; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel inputStyle` is scoped to one file and disagrees with two other input surfaces in the same module. The `settingsStyles` approach — a named export from a module-level constants file — is the pattern most easily extended across other modules without introducing a new component.
239	
240	2. **StatusBadge implementation — Settings `Import.tsx` token-pair pattern.** `Import.tsx` renders status badges using `background: var(--io-success-subtle)` + `color: var(--io-success)` (and equivalent pairs for other states). This requires no color math, no string interpolation, and adapts automatically to theme changes. It outperforms Console's hardcoded rgba hex arithmetic, Designer's fully hardcoded badge colors, and Settings' own `OpcSources` hex-alpha concatenation bug.
241	
242	3. **Button system naming — Settings `settingsStyles.ts`.** `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` are named, exported constants with clear semantic intent. Console has zero named button abstractions; Designer has `IconBtn` as a primitive but five unlabeled inline variations. Named variants provide a single place to fix token deviations (e.g., replacing `--io-text-on-accent` with `--io-accent-foreground`) across all usages simultaneously.
243	
244	4. **Page-level label structure — Settings `SettingsPageLayout.tsx`.** A dedicated shared component that consistently renders page title (`<h2>`, 18px/600, `--io-text-primary`), page description (13px, `--io-text-muted`), and optional action area. Console has no equivalent (the module name is raw inline text in `index.tsx`); Designer has no equivalent across its views. `SettingsPageLayout` ensures every Settings sub-page has a consistent heading hierarchy from a single component.
245	
246	5. **Menu implementation discipline — Settings.** Settings uses only `shared/components/ContextMenu` and nothing else for menus — one pattern, one source of truth for visual properties. Console adds one hand-rolled dropdown alongside it; Designer adds two custom dropdowns with divergent positioning, z-index, and shadow values. Settings demonstrates that the shared component is sufficient and that additional custom panels create maintenance surface.
247	
248	6. **Form label primitives — Designer `FieldLabel` component.** `DesignerRightPanel`'s `FieldLabel` is a `<label>` element (semantically correct, unlike `<div>`-based label spreads in Console and Settings), consistently applied throughout the file, with fixed values (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em/`--io-text-muted`). It is the only reusable label primitive that uses correct HTML semantics in any of the three modules, and would require minimal adaptation to become a cross-module shared component.
249	
250	7. **Destructive confirmation pattern — Settings `ConfirmDialog` usage.** Settings uses `shared/components/ConfirmDialog` for destructive actions across most pages (`Certificates`, `Email`, `BulkUpdate`, `AuthProviders`, `PointManagement`). Console implements three different inline confirmation dialogs; Designer implements per-dialog confirm flows with no shared primitive. Settings demonstrates that a shared confirmation dialog is sufficient for the full range of destructive actions in a complex module.
251

# UI Audit Comparison — Cross-Module Analysis

**Source files:** `ui-audit/01-console.md`, `ui-audit/01-designer.md`, `ui-audit/01-settings.md`
**Date:** 2026-05-27

---

## Category 1 — Color Palette and Theme Tokens

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## List 1 — Elements already consistent across all three modules

1. **Token registry authority.** All three modules draw colors exclusively from the `index.css` CSS custom property registry. No module owns or overrides token definitions.
2. **Typography implementation pattern.** All three use raw pixel integer literals in inline styles. None reference the 16 typography scale tokens (`--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`) defined in `index.css`.
3. **Shared ContextMenu component.** All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` as the sole or primary right-click context menu implementation. Visual properties for context menus are governed by the shared component.
4. **Primary button background.** All three use `var(--io-accent)` as the background of primary action buttons.
5. **Secondary button border.** All three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` as the border for secondary/outline button variants.
6. **`--io-btn-*` tokens unused.** All three modules ignore the six `--io-btn-bg`, `--io-btn-hover`, `--io-btn-active`, `--io-btn-text`, `--io-btn-secondary-bg`, `--io-btn-secondary-border` tokens defined in `index.css`.
7. **`--io-input-*` tokens largely unused.** Console and Settings ignore all five `--io-input-*` tokens; Designer's `ShapePointSelector` uses `--io-input-bg` (index.css:131) and `--io-input-border` (index.css:132). Three of the five tokens remain unused across all three modules.
8. **Focus ring suppression.** All three suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) on form inputs without applying a CSS replacement using `--io-focus-ring` or `--io-input-focus-border`.
9. **Hover via DOM mutation.** All three use `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.*`) for at least some interactive hover states rather than CSS `:hover` rules or React state.
10. **Connection state dot pattern.** All three represent connection state (WS connection, OPC source state) with 6–8px circular dots using `var(--io-success)` for connected and `var(--io-danger)` for error.
11. **`--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` unused.** `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are ignored by all three modules; `--io-modal-backdrop` IS defined (index.css:139) and IS used in ≥6 Settings files. Console and Designer do not use it.
12. **Hardcoded primary button text.** All three hardcode the text color on accent-background buttons (`#fff`, `#09090b`, or `var(--io-text-on-accent)` which is undefined) instead of using `var(--io-accent-foreground)`.

---

## List 2 — Elements that are inconsistent across modules

1. **Side panel background token.** Console and Settings both use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` for the panel background; Designer's left palette uses `var(--io-surface)` — one tier lighter.
2. **Modal backdrop color.** Console uses hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`; Designer uses the same range hardcoded; Settings uses five distinct specifications including one undefined token (`--io-overlay`) and one defined token (`--io-modal-backdrop`) with no consistent value among the three.
3. **Form input background token.** Settings canonical (`settingsStyles`) uses `var(--io-surface-sunken)`; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel` uses `var(--io-surface)`; Console uses `var(--io-surface-secondary)` or `var(--io-surface-elevated)` inconsistently — three different surface tiers across modules for the same element.
4. **Section / field label typography convention.** Console palette section labels are 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; Designer `FieldLabel` is 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em; Settings `labelStyle` is 12px/500 with no uppercase — same UI concept (label above a form field or palette section) implemented with three different size, weight, and transform combinations.
5. **StatusBadge implementation.** Console uses hardcoded hex-rgba arithmetic; Designer uses fully hardcoded colors for READ-ONLY badge; Settings has five incompatible implementations (one broken, one using `color-mix()`, one using token pairs, one dot-only, one dot+text).
6. **Modal accessibility (ARIA).** Console: three inline modals lack `role="dialog"`; Designer: only `RecognitionWizard` has ARIA; Settings: `RestorePreviewModal`, all `Import` dialogs, `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal` have no ARIA — each module has a different gap profile.
7. **Undefined tokens referenced.** All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones: Console references `--io-bg` and `--io-text`; Designer references `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-accent-muted`; Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, and `--io-accent-rgb` (genuinely undefined). Tokens previously listed for Settings that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (index.css:139), `--io-info` (index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (index.css:29).
8. **Button hover states.** Designer's `IconBtn` has a 0.1s transition to `var(--io-surface-elevated)` on hover; Console has no hover state on any toolbar or modal button; Settings has no hover or focus styles on any button variant.
9. **Toolbar structure.** Console has one 48px header bar; Designer has four stacked bars totaling 144px of chrome (36+44+36+28); Settings has no toolbar.
10. **Menu pattern consistency.** Settings uses only the shared `ContextMenu` component; Console uses it alongside one hand-rolled dropdown; Designer uses it alongside two custom dropdown implementations with different positioning strategies and z-index values.
11. **Modal z-index values.** Console: 9999/1001/4000; Designer: 1000–3000 (six distinct values); Settings: multiple with no registry — all uncoordinated with the `--io-z-modal: 300` token.
12. **Semantic heading elements.** Console uses no semantic heading elements anywhere; Designer uses `<h1>`/`<h2>`/`<h3>` in `SymbolLibrary` and `DesignerGraphicsList` but `<div>` elsewhere; Settings uses `<h2>` in `SettingsPageLayout` — no consistent policy across or within modules.

---

## List 3 — Elements where one module is the best version others could converge on

1. **Form input centralization — Settings via `settingsStyles.ts`.** `settingsStyles.ts` defines a single named `inputStyle` object that propagates automatically to all pages that import it. Console has no equivalent; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel inputStyle` is scoped to one file and disagrees with two other input surfaces in the same module. The `settingsStyles` approach — a named export from a module-level constants file — is the pattern most easily extended across other modules without introducing a new component.

2. **StatusBadge implementation — Settings `Import.tsx` token-pair pattern.** `Import.tsx` renders status badges using `background: var(--io-success-subtle)` + `color: var(--io-success)` (and equivalent pairs for other states). This requires no color math, no string interpolation, and adapts automatically to theme changes. It outperforms Console's hardcoded rgba hex arithmetic, Designer's fully hardcoded badge colors, and Settings' own `OpcSources` hex-alpha concatenation bug.

3. **Button system naming — Settings `settingsStyles.ts`.** `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` are named, exported constants with clear semantic intent. Console has zero named button abstractions; Designer has `IconBtn` as a primitive but five unlabeled inline variations. Named variants provide a single place to fix token deviations (e.g., replacing `--io-text-on-accent` with `--io-accent-foreground`) across all usages simultaneously.

4. **Page-level label structure — Settings `SettingsPageLayout.tsx`.** A dedicated shared component that consistently renders page title (`<h2>`, 18px/600, `--io-text-primary`), page description (13px, `--io-text-muted`), and optional action area. Console has no equivalent (the module name is raw inline text in `index.tsx`); Designer has no equivalent across its views. `SettingsPageLayout` ensures every Settings sub-page has a consistent heading hierarchy from a single component.

5. **Menu implementation discipline — Settings.** Settings uses only `shared/components/ContextMenu` and nothing else for menus — one pattern, one source of truth for visual properties. Console adds one hand-rolled dropdown alongside it; Designer adds two custom dropdowns with divergent positioning, z-index, and shadow values. Settings demonstrates that the shared component is sufficient and that additional custom panels create maintenance surface.

6. **Form label primitives — Designer `FieldLabel` component.** `DesignerRightPanel`'s `FieldLabel` is a `<label>` element (semantically correct, unlike `<div>`-based label spreads in Console and Settings), consistently applied throughout the file, with fixed values (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em/`--io-text-muted`). It is the only reusable label primitive that uses correct HTML semantics in any of the three modules, and would require minimal adaptation to become a cross-module shared component.

7. **Destructive confirmation pattern — Settings `ConfirmDialog` usage.** Settings uses `shared/components/ConfirmDialog` for destructive actions across most pages (`Certificates`, `Email`, `BulkUpdate`, `AuthProviders`, `PointManagement`). Console implements three different inline confirmation dialogs; Designer implements per-dialog confirm flows with no shared primitive. Settings demonstrates that a shared confirmation dialog is sufficient for the full range of destructive actions in a complex module.

---

1	# UI Audit Recommendations

6	---
7	
8	## Section 1 — Target Architecture
9	
10	The user's working hypothesis stated three claims. Each is evaluated against the audit evidence.
11	
12	### Claim A: App shell (top bar, left nav, routing, theme) should be shared across all modules
13	
14	**Supported — partially already true, partially aspirational.**
15	
16	All three modules draw from the same `index.css` 138-token registry (Cat 1, List 1 Item 1). The `shared/components/ContextMenu` component, `VersionRecoveryDialog`/`SaveConfirmDialog`, and `ConfirmDialog` are already cross-module shared infrastructure (Cat 4 List 1 Item 3; Cat 11 notes). The AppShell sidebar design pattern (`--io-sidebar-width: 240px`, active left-border accent) exists and is implemented, but all three modules deviate from it with hardcoded 220px and missing active indicators (Cat 5, Deviations). The token registry is the correct shared foundation; the gap is that modules define their own overrides, reference undefined tokens, and ignore defined tokens for height, modal backdrop, z-index, and button styles.
17	
18	**Refined target:** The app shell provides the token registry, routing, top bar, sidebar chrome, and a small set of shared primitive components. Modules consume these without redefining them. No module defines its own token namespace; undefined token references are zero; every module uses the same constants for sidebar width, modal backdrop, and z-index stacking.
19	
20	### Claim B: Each module should share an underlying framework implemented uniformly
21	
22	**Partially supported — the right framing is a thin shared-constants layer, not a component library.**
23	
24	The audit found no shared Button component, no shared Input component, no shared Dialog wrapper, and no shared form label component spanning all three modules. However, it also found that the `settingsStyles.ts` approach — a named export from a module-level constants file — is already the closest to a shared standard and is the pattern most easily extended (Cat 7, List 1; Cat 6, List 3 Items 1, 3). Designer's `IconBtn` and `FieldLabel` are module-local reusable primitives that would require minimal adaptation to promote to `shared/components/` (Cat 6; Cat 9, List 3 Item 6).
25	
26	A heavyweight component library is not warranted given the app's scale. The evidence supports a **thin shared-constants and shared-primitives layer**: a small file of style constant objects (`btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `inputStyle`, `labelStyle`) plus two or three React components (`FieldLabel`, `StatusBadge`, `Dialog`) promoted into `shared/components/`. Hover and focus states should be CSS-driven rather than DOM-mutation-driven (Cat 2 List 1 Item 9), which is achievable with a small `shared/styles/` Tailwind-class or CSS-module approach without a full component system.
27	
28	**Refined target:** A `shared/styles/` constants file and no more than four new shared components replace the per-module duplicates. No module defines its own button system, input style, or dialog backdrop independently.
29	
30	### Claim C: The main canvas/work area should share a rendering engine with per-module functionality layered on top
31	
32	**Partially supported with a critical clarification about what "rendering engine" means.**
33	
34	The shared graphics layer (`SceneRenderer`, `TimeSeriesChart`/`ChartRenderer`, `DataTable`, and the CSS files `alarmFlash.css`, `operationalState.css`, `lod.css`) is already a shared rendering engine consumed inside Console panes. This is working correctly and should continue (Cat 10, Shared Infrastructure entries). The evidence does NOT support merging the work-surface containers: Console's `WorkspaceGrid` (react-grid-layout tile dashboard) and Designer's `DesignerCanvas.tsx` (12,067-line SVG editor with FSM interaction model) serve fundamentally different work modes and share no meaningful interaction semantics (Cat 10, Implementation). Converging the containers would introduce false coupling without shared benefit.
35	
36	**Refined target:** The shared rendering engine exists and should be maintained. The work-surface container is intentionally module-specific. The correct convergence work in Cat 10 is fixing bugs in the shared infrastructure (selection token prefix bug, alarmFlash.css theme hardcoding) and ensuring each module's canvas follows the same token conventions for its local chrome — not merging the containers.
37

40	### Consolidated target architecture statement
41	
42	When this work is complete:
43	
44	1. The `index.css` token registry is the sole source for all color, spacing, radius, shadow, and z-index values. No undefined tokens are referenced anywhere in the frontend. No module defines its own token namespace.
45	
46	2. A `shared/styles/` constants file provides named style objects for buttons, inputs, and field labels used uniformly across Console, Designer, and Settings. No module duplicates a button or input style system independently.
47	
48	3. Four shared components exist in `shared/components/`: `FieldLabel` (promoted from Designer), `StatusBadge` (promoted from Settings Import pattern), `Dialog` (wrapper with ARIA, `--io-modal-backdrop`, and a coordinated z-index), and `ConfirmDialog` (already exists; usage extended to all modules).
49	
50	4. The shared graphics rendering layer (`SceneRenderer`, the CSS infrastructure files) is maintained as-is. The work-surface containers (WorkspaceGrid, DesignerCanvas) remain module-specific.
51	
52	5. All interactive elements have visible focus indicators. DOM-mutation hover (`onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` style writes) is replaced with CSS `:hover` rules or React state.

156	- Fix: Align Designer left palette background: `var(--io-surface)` → `var(--io-surface-secondary)` to match Console and Settings
157	- Fix: Resolve `--io-sidebar-width` discrepancy — either update the token to 220px to match current practice, or update all three modules to 240px. Pick one and be consistent.
158	- Fix: Settings active nav item — add `borderLeft: 2px solid var(--io-accent)` on active state to match AppShell pattern
159	- Fix: Replace DOM-mutation hover in Console palette (`e.currentTarget.style.*`) with CSS `:hover` or React state
160	- Fix: Designer `CanvasLayerRow` `--io-surface-raised` undefined → use `var(--io-surface-elevated)`
161	- Fix: Section label typography — standardize on 11px/600/uppercase/0.06em per Cat 2 table (Console and Designer already agree; bring Settings nav group into alignment)
162	
163	**Build new:** None for panel containers. Shared `FieldLabel` (Cat 9) and `StatusBadge` (Cat 8) are the shared components this category depends on.
164	
165	---
166	
167	### Category 6 — Buttons
168	
169	**Standardize on:** `settingsStyles.ts` named-variant pattern as the starting point. Promote to `shared/styles/buttons.ts` accessible across all modules.
170	
171	**Adopt:** The four named variants `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` with corrected token references. Do not build a React `<Button>` component — named style constant objects are sufficient and the existing patterns already use this approach.
172	
173	**Actions:**
174	- Create: `frontend/src/shared/styles/buttons.ts` with corrected variants:
175	  - `btnPrimary`: `var(--io-accent)` bg, `var(--io-accent-foreground)` text (replaces `--io-text-on-accent` and hardcoded `#fff`/`#09090b`), `var(--io-radius)`, add hover state via `transition: opacity 0.1s` or `var(--io-btn-hover)` token
176	  - `btnSecondary`: transparent bg, `var(--io-text-secondary)` text, `1px solid var(--io-border)`, `var(--io-radius)`, matching font-weight (600)
177	  - `btnDanger`: same structure as secondary but `var(--io-danger)` text and border
178	  - `btnSmall`: size modifier only
179	- Migrate: Console toolbar + modal buttons to the shared file
180	- Migrate: Designer text-action buttons and Stencil/Import primary buttons to the shared file; leave `IconBtn` as-is (it is already a good local primitive)
181	- Merge: `BulkUpdate.tsx` `BTN_PRIMARY`/etc. → import from shared file
182	- Fix: Designer StatusBar segments `<div onClick>` → `<button>` (see Cat 3)
183	- Fix: Add CSS hover states to all button variants; remove all `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` style mutations on buttons
184	- Fix: `DesignerImport` fallback `#3b82f6` primary button background → `var(--io-accent)`
185	- Fix: All destructive button inconsistency in Designer (ghost rgba vs solid fill) → standardize on `btnDanger` solid pattern
186	
187	**Build new:** `shared/styles/buttons.ts` (constants file, not a component).
188	
189	---
190	
191	### Category 7 — Form Inputs
192	
193	**Standardize on:** `settingsStyles.ts inputStyle` pattern. Same approach as buttons: promote to `shared/styles/inputs.ts`.
194	
195	**Adopt:** Standard input object: `background: var(--io-surface-sunken)`, `border: 1px solid var(--io-border)`, `borderRadius: var(--io-radius)`, `padding: 8px 10px`, `fontSize: 13px`, `color: var(--io-text-primary)`. Use `--io-input-bg` and `--io-input-border` tokens where applicable (Designer's ShapePointSelector already does this correctly and can serve as validation that the tokens work).
196	
197	**Actions:**
198	- Create: `frontend/src/shared/styles/inputs.ts` with `inputStyle` and `labelStyle` objects
199	- Migrate: Console `PaneConfigModal`, `PaneWrapper`, `ConsolePalette` inputs to shared inputStyle
200	- Migrate: Designer `DesignerRightPanel inputStyle`, `PointPickerModal` inputs to shared inputStyle
201	- Migrate: Settings `Import.tsx`, `BulkUpdate.tsx`, `Sessions.tsx` (the current diverging cases) to the shared inputStyle
202	- Fix (accessibility — all modules): Remove `outline: none` and replace with `outline: 2px solid var(--io-accent)` on `:focus-visible`. This is a wide change; use the shared constants file so it's fixed in one place.
203	- Fix: `fontFamily: monospace` → `var(--io-font-mono)` in Console `PaneConfigModal` and Settings `Groups.tsx`
204	- Fix: `AuthProviders.tsx` — remove the `<style>` tag injected for checkbox `accent-color`; use the `accentColor: "var(--io-accent)"` inline style already used by other pages
205	- Keep: Designer `ThemedColorSelect` as a module-local specialized component (restricts to ISA-101 token pairs — this is domain logic, not a shared concern)
206	
207	**Build new:** `shared/styles/inputs.ts` (constants file, not a component).
208	
209	---
210	
211	### Category 8 — Status Indicators
212	
213	**Standardize on:** Settings `Import.tsx` token-pair pattern (`background: var(--io-success-subtle)`, `color: var(--io-success)`) as the canonical StatusBadge implementation.
214	
215	**Build new:** `shared/components/StatusBadge.tsx` — a simple component accepting `status` (connected/disconnected/error/warning/info/running/etc.) and optional `label` props, rendering with the correct token pair for each state. This is the one new component where a React component (not just a constants object) is warranted, because the rendering logic (which token pair, whether to show dot vs pill vs text) is non-trivial and repeated five different ways in Settings alone.
216	
217	**Actions:**
218	- Fix (functional regression): `OpcSources StatusBadge` hex-alpha concat bug — `${color}20` → `color-mix(in srgb, ${color} 12%, transparent)` or migrate to shared `StatusBadge` component
219	- Migrate: All five Settings StatusBadge implementations → shared component
220	- Migrate: Console connection dots and priority/state/quality badges → shared component or token-based inline styles
221	- Migrate: Designer WS dot, dirty indicator, READ-ONLY badge → use token colors (minimum); migrate to shared component where appropriate
222	- Fix: Designer TEST MODE `<style>` tag injection → CSS class + token-based keyframe, remove the inline `<style>` element
223	- Fix: Console published dot `#10b981` → `var(--io-success)`
224	- Fix: All connection dot glow shadows `#22c55e` → `var(--io-success)` (or remove glow if it introduces complexity)
225	- Fix: `alarmFlash.css` — migrate `#ef4444`, `#f97316`, `#eab308`, `#f4f4f5`, `#60a5fa` to `--io-alarm-urgent`, `--io-alarm-high`, `--io-alarm-low`, `--io-alarm-diagnostic`, `--io-alarm-custom` tokens. This is the correct fix for theme adaptation; the off-state `#808080` has no token equivalent and should be defined (`--io-alarm-inactive: #808080`).
226	
227	**Do not change:** `operationalState.css` — ISA-101 hardcoded colors are intentional and documented.
228	
229	---
230	
231	### Category 9 — Labels and Headers
232	
233	**Standardize on:** Two shared components to promote:
234	1. `SettingsPageLayout.tsx` for page-level heading (already a shared component within Settings; extend access to Console and Designer views)
235	2. `FieldLabel` from `DesignerRightPanel` — promote to `shared/components/FieldLabel.tsx`
236	
237	**Actions:**
238	- Promote: `DesignerRightPanel.tsx` `FieldLabel` (lines 201–203) → `shared/components/FieldLabel.tsx`. Minimal changes: standardize size to 11px (from 10px) to align with the Cat 2 table convention; keep other properties (`/600/uppercase/0.05em/--io-text-muted`).
239	- Adopt: `FieldLabel` in Console `PaneConfigModal` and Settings pages to replace inline `<div>`-based form labels
240	- Adopt: `SettingsPageLayout` pattern — evaluate extending it to Console's `PaneConfigModal` header and Designer's view-level headers as applicable
241	- Fix: `var(--io-text)` undefined in Console/Designer dialog titles → `var(--io-text-primary)`
242	- Fix: Inconsistent `letterSpacing` across uppercase labels → standardize on 0.06em
243	- Fix: Console — no semantic heading elements anywhere; add `<h2>` for page/section titles where appropriate
244	- Fix: Designer `SymbolLibrary` — `<h2>` elements with inconsistent sizes (15px/600 vs 16px/700 within same file) → pick one and apply consistently
245	- Do not change: Settings `SettingsPageLayout` `<h2>` page title — correct semantic for a sub-page context
246	
247	---
248	
249	### Category 10 — Canvas / Main Work Area
250	
251	**Standardize on:** Module-specific canvas containers are correct and should not be merged. Shared graphics infrastructure should be the focus.
252	
253	**Actions (by priority):**
254	
255	- **Fixed 2026-05-27** (functional regression, was highest priority): `shared/clipboard/selection/selection.css` and `MarqueeLayer.tsx` — `var(--accent)` → `var(--io-accent)` (selection.css lines 2 and 9; MarqueeLayer.tsx line 101). `rgba(80,180,255,0.08)` → `var(--io-accent-subtle)` (MarqueeLayer.tsx line 100). Selection highlight and marquee border now render with teal accent color.
256	- Fix: `alarmFlash.css` — migrate alarm hex colors to `--io-alarm-*` tokens (see Cat 8). Required for light/HPHMI theme support.
257	- Fix: Console `WorkspaceGrid` container `var(--io-bg)` (undefined) → `var(--io-surface-primary)` (after defining `--io-bg` alias in Cat 1 token work, this becomes automatic)
258	- Fix: DesignerCanvas canvas border `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` → define a token or use `var(--io-border)` with opacity; grid lines `rgba(128,128,128,0.12/0.28)` → acceptable to leave as-is if theme support is not required for the grid appearance
259	- Fix: DesignerCanvas resize handles `fill="white"` → `fill="var(--io-text-inverse)"` or keep `white` with a formal comment documenting it as intentional in dark-theme-only contexts
260	- Fix: `--io-error` references in DesignerCanvas → `--io-danger` (after defining `--io-error` alias in Cat 1 token work, this becomes automatic)
261	- Fix: Guide line colors (`rgba(0,200,255,0.5)`, `rgba(255,160,0,0.5)`) — low priority, canvas-only; acceptable as-is if guide colors are intentional design choices
262	- Fix: "Paste as…" submenu items for `table`/`temporary-graphic` always render disabled — separate bug, out of scope for UI consistency work but worth a task file
263	
264	**Do not change:** `lod.css` (no deviations), `operationalState.css` (intentional exception).
265	
266	---
267	
268	### Category 11 — Modals and Dialogs
269	
270	**Standardize on:** Build one shared `Dialog` wrapper component. Radix Dialog (already used in Settings for PointManagement, AuthProviders, Email) is the best implementation and should be the standard for new dialogs. The shared wrapper provides: `--io-modal-backdrop` backdrop, `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal="true"`, `background: var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `borderRadius: var(--io-radius-lg)`, and a coordinated z-index.
271	
272	**Actions:**
273	
274	- Fix (token): Standardize z-index values. `--io-z-modal: 300` is misaligned with actual usage (1000–9999). Either raise the token to 1000 minimum, or define a z-index scale (`--io-z-dropdown: 200`, `--io-z-modal: 1000`, `--io-z-toast: 2000`). Do not leave the token at 300 when no dialog uses it.
275	- Fix (token): `--io-modal-backdrop` is already defined and used in ≥6 Settings files. Console and Designer should migrate to it from hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`.
276	- Fix (accessibility, urgent): `RestorePreviewModal.tsx` — add `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal="true"`. This is the highest-priority ARIA gap because it appears on a destructive-adjacent action path.
277	- Fix (accessibility): Console inline modals (3 dialogs in `index.tsx`) — add `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal`.
278	- Fix (accessibility): Settings `Import.tsx` Modal/Drawer and `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal` — add ARIA.
279	- Fix: Replace all 8 `window.confirm()` calls with `ConfirmDialog`:
280	  - OpcSources.tsx (3 calls)
281	  - Import.tsx (3 calls)
282	  - CameraStreams.tsx (1 call)
283	  - SupplementalConnectorsTab.tsx (1 call)
284	- Fix: Standardize `borderRadius` to `var(--io-radius-lg)` across all modals (currently 8, 9, 10, 12px scattered)
285	- Fix: Standardize modal content background to `var(--io-surface-elevated)` (currently mixed with `--io-surface`, `--io-surface-secondary`, `--io-surface-primary`)
286	- Fix: Designer primary button text in dialogs — `#09090b`/`#fff` hardcoded → `var(--io-accent-foreground)` (after shared buttons.ts is in place, this is automatic)
287	- Fix: Designer error color mixing (`--io-alarm-high` for errors in wrong domain, `--io-error` undefined) → use `--io-danger` consistently
288	- Fix: Designer step indicator inconsistency across 5 wizard dialogs — define one step indicator pattern (not blocked by other work but needs its own task)
289	
290	---
291	
292	## Section 3 — Rough Migration Order
293	
294	The dependency structure has four phases. Items within a phase are largely independent of each other.
295	
296	### Phase 1 — Token registry (unblocks everything downstream)
297	
298	No component changes. Pure token additions to `index.css`.
299	
300	1. Define missing tokens: `--io-bg`, `--io-text`, `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-text-on-accent` (alias to `--io-accent-foreground`), `--io-error` (alias to `--io-danger`), `--io-alarm-inactive: #808080`, `--io-text-inverse`.
301	2. Fix z-index token: raise `--io-z-modal` to a realistic value (recommend 1000); optionally define `--io-z-dropdown: 500` and `--io-z-toast: 2000`.
302	3. Resolve sidebar width: pick 220px or 240px; update token or code to match.
303	
304	**Why first:** Every downstream fix that replaces hardcoded hex or undefined token references becomes a one-word change once these tokens exist. Without them, each fix requires two changes (add the token AND update the reference).
305	
306	### Phase 2 — Functional regressions (urgent; no dependency on Phase 1 being complete)
307	
308	These are bugs visible at runtime, not polish issues.
309	
310	1. ~~`selection.css` + `MarqueeLayer.tsx`: `var(--accent)` → `var(--io-accent)`, `rgba(80,180,255,0.08)` → `var(--io-accent-subtle)`. (2-file change.)~~ **Fixed 2026-05-27.**
311	2. ~~`OpcSources StatusBadge` hex-alpha concat bug: `${color}20` → `color-mix(...)` or component migration.~~ **Fixed 2026-05-27.** `background: \`${color}20\`` → `color-mix(in srgb, ${color} 12%, transparent)`; `border: \`1px solid ${color}40\`` → `color-mix(in srgb, ${color} 25%, transparent)`. Fix scoped to the local `StatusBadge` function in `OpcSources.tsx` (lines 168–170); matches the `color-mix` pattern already used by `SystemHealth.tsx`. **Note:** The same `${color}20`/`${color}40` hex-alpha pattern also appears in `Users.tsx` (Badge, line 108/110), `Roles.tsx` (Badge, line 51/53), `CameraStreams.tsx` (line 785/787), and `MaintenanceTicketsPanel.tsx` (line 52) — these were not fixed here and remain broken when CSS variable strings are passed as the color argument.
312	3. `alarmFlash.css`: migrate hex to `--io-alarm-*` tokens. (This affects theme support, not current dark-theme functionality, but the hardcoded values are wrong in principle and block HPHMI work.)
313	4. Critical ARIA fixes: `RestorePreviewModal` + Console inline modals + Import/OpcSources dialogs. (Accessibility regression on destructive paths.)
314	5. Replace 8 `window.confirm()` calls with `ConfirmDialog`.
315	6. Console duplicate Publish button — remove one render block.
316	
317	**Why second:** These are observable regressions. They are small-scope, can be reviewed independently, and should not wait for the broader convergence work.
318	
319	### Phase 3 — Shared style constants (enables consistent convergence without component work)
320	
321	Depends on: Phase 1 token fixes (so constants reference valid tokens).
322	
323	1. Create `shared/styles/buttons.ts` with corrected `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` variants.
324	2. Create `shared/styles/inputs.ts` with standard `inputStyle` (including focus ring; remove `outline: none`).
325	3. Migrate Console button and input inline styles to shared constants.
326	4. Migrate Designer text-action buttons and import buttons to `buttons.ts`; migrate `DesignerRightPanel inputStyle` and `PointPickerModal` to `inputs.ts`.
327	5. Migrate Settings `Import.tsx`, `BulkUpdate.tsx`, `Sessions.tsx` diverging buttons and inputs to shared constants.
328	6. Fix `accentColor` / font-family regressions (OpcSources `<style>` tag, monospace font references) as part of input migration.
329	
330	**Unblocks:** Phase 4 modal work (button styles inside dialogs) and Cat 6/7 cleanup across all modules.
331	
332	### Phase 4 — Shared components (highest coordination cost)
333	
334	Depends on: Phase 3 constants (dialogs need buttons; StatusBadge needs token pairs from Phase 1).
335	
336	1. Promote `FieldLabel` to `shared/components/FieldLabel.tsx`. Migrate usage in Console and Settings.
337	2. Build `shared/components/StatusBadge.tsx`. Migrate all five Settings implementations, Console priority/state/quality badges, Designer badge indicators.
338	3. Build `shared/components/Dialog.tsx` (thin wrapper: ARIA, backdrop token, z-index, standard container styles). Migrate Console inline modals and Designer non-Radix dialogs to use it.
339	4. Fix Designer TEST MODE `<style>` tag injection → CSS keyframe class.
340	5. Extend `SettingsPageLayout` accessibility or create a simpler page-title shared component for Console and Designer views.
341	
342	### Phase 5 — Polish and alignment (deferred; no functional impact)
343	
344	Depends on: Phases 1–4 being stable.
345	
346	1. Typography: standardize on documented size/weight/spacing values across section labels, field labels, tab labels, page titles.
347	2. Toolbar heights: define `--io-toolbar-height` token; update Designer skeleton placeholder.
348	3. Hover/focus: replace remaining DOM-mutation hover with CSS `:hover` pseudo-classes or React state.
349	4. DesignerCanvas grid line colors, canvas border, guide line colors: token-ify or formally document as intentional.
350	5. Semantic headings: add `<h2>`/`<h3>` where currently bare `<div>`.
351	6. Section label `letterSpacing` standardization to 0.06em.
352	
353	---
354	
355	## Section 4 — Risks and Unknowns
356	
357	### R1 — `--io-z-modal: 300` is unusable as-is
358	
359	Every dialog in all three modules uses z-index values between 1000 and 9999. Raising the token will fix any code that correctly references it, but code currently using hardcoded integers (the majority) will be unaffected until individually migrated. There is a risk that the migration is incomplete and layers will stack incorrectly in edge cases (e.g., a console modal rendered inside a designer tab). A z-index audit across all `zIndex` values in the frontend would be needed to produce a safe z-index scale before setting definitive token values.
360	
361	### R2 — Light theme and HPHMI theme are not yet implemented; audit was dark-theme-only
362	
363	The audit verified token references but could not test whether visual output is correct in non-dark themes. `alarmFlash.css` is confirmed dark-theme-only (colors are hardcoded hex matching dark-theme token values). `operationalState.css` is intentionally non-adaptive. Any module-local hardcoded hex values will also fail to adapt. If theme switching is a near-term requirement, the Phase 2 alarmFlash fix and Phase 1 token additions should be front-loaded.
364	
365	### R3 — DesignerCanvas.tsx at 12,067 lines is high-risk to touch
366	
367	Multiple Phase 4 items (Dialog promotion, token fixes) require changes inside or adjacent to DesignerCanvas. Any change here should be minimally invasive — prefer aliasing tokens (Phase 1) over in-file search-and-replace of hardcoded values, since the token alias approach reduces the number of touch points in this file.
368	
369	### R4 — `window.confirm()` replacements require confirming render context
370	
371	Eight `window.confirm()` calls need to become `ConfirmDialog` renders. Some are inside contexts (OpcSources, Import) that render large tables or multi-step wizard flows. Each replacement needs to verify that the `ConfirmDialog` can be portal-rendered correctly from the calling component's position in the tree, since some callers may be inside transformed ancestors (CLAUDE.md invariant: `position: fixed` inside react-grid-layout transforms breaks — use `createPortal`).
372	
373	### R5 — BulkUpdate.tsx is a parallel button system inside Settings
374	
375	`BulkUpdate.tsx` defines its own full button style set (`BTN_PRIMARY`, `BTN_SECONDARY`, `BTN_DANGER`) independent of `settingsStyles.ts`. Before cross-module button standardization is clean, this file needs to be migrated to the shared constants. If it is overlooked, Settings will still have two competing button systems internally, undermining the shared approach.
376	
377	### R6 — Audit scope was limited to Console, Designer, and Settings
378	
379	Other frontend modules (OPC config detail views, camera streams, supplemental connectors, display element config panels, SAML/auth provider pages) were not audited. The undefined token references (`--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, `--io-accent-rgb`) and `window.confirm()` calls found in Settings suggest that scope extensions would find additional deviations. Phase 1 token fixes will automatically resolve references to newly-defined tokens across all modules; component migrations would require a separate audit pass.
380	
381	### R7 — Focus ring removal is a wide-scope accessibility regression
382	
383	Every interactive element in all three modules suppresses the native focus ring via `outline: none` with no CSS replacement. Fixing this requires touching every input, button, and interactive div — hundreds of instances. The shared constants files (Phase 3) provide the leverage point to fix this in one place for buttons and inputs, but custom interactive elements (palette tiles, canvas nodes, tab strips, status bar segments) need individual fixes. A full accessibility pass scoped to just focus states may be warranted as a standalone workstream.
384	
385	### R8 — Audit detail limitations in specific areas
386	
387	The audit did not include source-level review of:
388	- `CategoryShapeWizard.tsx`, `ShapeDropDialog.tsx`, `RecognitionWizard.tsx`, `IographicImportWizard.tsx` (Designer) — only top-level structural facts were captured; step indicator patterns were noted as inconsistent but not enumerated
389	- Camera streams and supplemental connectors (Settings) — flagged for `window.confirm()` calls only
390	- Report scheduling, SMS providers, EULA admin (Settings) — referenced in the reconciliation log as using `--io-modal-backdrop` correctly; not fully audited
391	
392	These gaps mean the "consistent deviations" counts may understate actual scope.
393

# UI Audit Recommendations

**Derived from:** `ui-audit/02-comparison.md` (post-reconciliation, authoritative)
**Date:** 2026-05-27

---

## Section 1 — Target Architecture

The user's working hypothesis stated three claims. Each is evaluated against the audit evidence.

### Claim A: App shell (top bar, left nav, routing, theme) should be shared across all modules

**Supported — partially already true, partially aspirational.**

All three modules draw from the same `index.css` 138-token registry (Cat 1, List 1 Item 1). The `shared/components/ContextMenu` component, `VersionRecoveryDialog`/`SaveConfirmDialog`, and `ConfirmDialog` are already cross-module shared infrastructure (Cat 4 List 1 Item 3; Cat 11 notes). The AppShell sidebar design pattern (`--io-sidebar-width: 240px`, active left-border accent) exists and is implemented, but all three modules deviate from it with hardcoded 220px and missing active indicators (Cat 5, Deviations). The token registry is the correct shared foundation; the gap is that modules define their own overrides, reference undefined tokens, and ignore defined tokens for height, modal backdrop, z-index, and button styles.

**Refined target:** The app shell provides the token registry, routing, top bar, sidebar chrome, and a small set of shared primitive components. Modules consume these without redefining them. No module defines its own token namespace; undefined token references are zero; every module uses the same constants for sidebar width, modal backdrop, and z-index stacking.

### Claim B: Each module should share an underlying framework implemented uniformly

**Partially supported — the right framing is a thin shared-constants layer, not a component library.**

The audit found no shared Button component, no shared Input component, no shared Dialog wrapper, and no shared form label component spanning all three modules. However, it also found that the `settingsStyles.ts` approach — a named export from a module-level constants file — is already the closest to a shared standard and is the pattern most easily extended (Cat 7, List 1; Cat 6, List 3 Items 1, 3). Designer's `IconBtn` and `FieldLabel` are module-local reusable primitives that would require minimal adaptation to promote to `shared/components/` (Cat 6; Cat 9, List 3 Item 6).

A heavyweight component library is not warranted given the app's scale. The evidence supports a **thin shared-constants and shared-primitives layer**: a small file of style constant objects (`btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `inputStyle`, `labelStyle`) plus two or three React components (`FieldLabel`, `StatusBadge`, `Dialog`) promoted into `shared/components/`. Hover and focus states should be CSS-driven rather than DOM-mutation-driven (Cat 2 List 1 Item 9), which is achievable with a small `shared/styles/` Tailwind-class or CSS-module approach without a full component system.

**Refined target:** A `shared/styles/` constants file and no more than four new shared components replace the per-module duplicates. No module defines its own button system, input style, or dialog backdrop independently.

### Claim C: The main canvas/work area should share a rendering engine with per-module functionality layered on top

**Partially supported with a critical clarification about what "rendering engine" means.**

The shared graphics layer (`SceneRenderer`, `TimeSeriesChart`/`ChartRenderer`, `DataTable`, and the CSS files `alarmFlash.css`, `operationalState.css`, `lod.css`) is already a shared rendering engine consumed inside Console panes. This is working correctly and should continue (Cat 10, Shared Infrastructure entries). The evidence does NOT support merging the work-surface containers: Console's `WorkspaceGrid` (react-grid-layout tile dashboard) and Designer's `DesignerCanvas.tsx` (12,067-line SVG editor with FSM interaction model) serve fundamentally different work modes and share no meaningful interaction semantics (Cat 10, Implementation). Converging the containers would introduce false coupling without shared benefit.

**Refined target:** The shared rendering engine exists and should be maintained. The work-surface container is intentionally module-specific. The correct convergence work in Cat 10 is fixing bugs in the shared infrastructure (selection token prefix bug, alarmFlash.css theme hardcoding) and ensuring each module's canvas follows the same token conventions for its local chrome — not merging the containers.

---

### Consolidated target architecture statement

When this work is complete:

1. The `index.css` token registry is the sole source for all color, spacing, radius, shadow, and z-index values. No undefined tokens are referenced anywhere in the frontend. No module defines its own token namespace.

2. A `shared/styles/` constants file provides named style objects for buttons, inputs, and field labels used uniformly across Console, Designer, and Settings. No module duplicates a button or input style system independently.

3. Four shared components exist in `shared/components/`: `FieldLabel` (promoted from Designer), `StatusBadge` (promoted from Settings Import pattern), `Dialog` (wrapper with ARIA, `--io-modal-backdrop`, and a coordinated z-index), and `ConfirmDialog` (already exists; usage extended to all modules).

4. The shared graphics rendering layer (`SceneRenderer`, the CSS infrastructure files) is maintained as-is. The work-surface containers (WorkspaceGrid, DesignerCanvas) remain module-specific.

5. All interactive elements have visible focus indicators. DOM-mutation hover (`onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` style writes) is replaced with CSS `:hover` rules or React state.

---

## Section 2 — Per-Element Convergence Recommendations

### Category 1 — Color Palette and Theme Tokens

**Standardize on:** The existing `index.css` 138-token registry. No changes to the registry structure; only fix what is broken in the token graph.

**Actions:**

- **Define missing tokens** — add to `index.css`:

**What to standardize now:** Document a fixed set of semantic size/weight values for recurring label contexts. Adopt these values uniformly rather than letting each module drift:

| Form field label | 11px / 600 / uppercase / 0.05em / `--io-text-muted` | Designer `FieldLabel` (after minor size alignment) |
| Tab label active / inactive | 13px / 600 / 400 | Console + Designer tab strips agree |
| Table column header | 11px / 600 / uppercase / 0.06em | Console + Settings agree |

### Category 10 — Canvas / Main Work Area

**Standardize on:** Module-specific canvas containers are correct and should not be merged. Shared graphics infrastructure should be the focus.

Selection highlight and marquee border now render with teal accent color.
- Fix: `alarmFlash.css` — migrate alarm hex colors to `--io-alarm-*` tokens (see Cat 8). Required for light/HPHMI theme support.
- Fix: Console `WorkspaceGrid` container `var(--io-bg)` (undefined) → `var(--io-surface-primary)` (after defining `--io-bg` alias in Cat 1 token work, this becomes automatic)

- Fix: DesignerCanvas resize handles `fill="white"` → `fill="var(--io-text-inverse)"` or keep `white` with a formal comment documenting it as intentional in dark-theme-only contexts

### Category 11 — Modals and Dialogs

**Standardize on:** Build one shared `Dialog` wrapper component. Radix Dialog (already used in Settings for PointManagement, AuthProviders, Email) is the best implementation and should be the standard for new dialogs. The shared wrapper provides: `--io-modal-backdrop` backdrop, `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal="true"`, `background: var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `borderRadius: var(--io-radius-lg)`, and a coordinated z-index.

**Actions:**

- Fix (token): Standardize z-index values. `--io-z-modal: 300` is misaligned with actual usage (1000–9999). Either raise the token to 1000 minimum, or define a z-index scale (`--io-z-dropdown: 200`, `--io-z-modal: 1000`, `--io-z-toast: 2000`). Do not leave the token at 300 when no dialog uses it.
- Fix (token): `--io-modal-backdrop` is already defined and used in ≥6 Settings files. Console and Designer should migrate to it from hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`.
- Fix (accessibility, urgent): `RestorePreviewModal.tsx` — add `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal="true"`. This is the highest-priority ARIA gap because it appears on a destructive-adjacent action path.
- Fix (accessibility): Console inline modals (3 dialogs in `index.tsx`) — add `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal`.
- Fix (accessibility): Settings `Import.tsx` Modal/Drawer and `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal` — add ARIA.
- Fix: Replace all 8 `window.confirm()` calls with `ConfirmDialog`:
  - OpcSources.tsx (3 calls)
  - Import.tsx (3 calls)
  - CameraStreams.tsx (1 call)
  - SupplementalConnectorsTab.tsx (1 call)
- Fix: Standardize `borderRadius` to `var(--io-radius-lg)` across all modals (currently 8, 9, 10, 12px scattered)
- Fix: Standardize modal content background to `var(--io-surface-elevated)` (currently mixed with `--io-surface`, `--io-surface-secondary`, `--io-surface-primary`)
- Fix: Designer primary button text in dialogs — `#09090b`/`#fff` hardcoded → `var(--io-accent-foreground)` (after shared buttons.ts is in place, this is automatic)
- Fix: Designer error color mixing (`--io-alarm-high` for errors in wrong domain, `--io-error` undefined) → use `--io-danger` consistently
- Fix: Designer step indicator inconsistency across 5 wizard dialogs — define one step indicator pattern (not blocked by other work but needs its own task)

---

## Section 3 — Rough Migration Order

The dependency structure has four phases. Items within a phase are largely independent of each other.

### Phase 1 — Token registry (unblocks everything downstream)

No component changes. Pure token additions to `index.css`.

1. Define missing tokens: `--io-bg`, `--io-text`, `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-text-on-accent` (alias to `--io-accent-foreground`), `--io-error` (alias to `--io-danger`), `--io-alarm-inactive: #808080`, `--io-text-inverse`.
2. Fix z-index token: raise `--io-z-modal` to a realistic value (recommend 1000); optionally define `--io-z-dropdown: 500` and `--io-z-toast: 2000`.
3. Resolve sidebar width: pick 220px or 240px; update token or code to match.

**Why first:** Every downstream fix that replaces hardcoded hex or undefined token references becomes a one-word change once these tokens exist. Without them, each fix requires two changes (add the token AND update the reference).

### Phase 2 — Functional regressions (urgent; no dependency on Phase 1 being complete)

These are bugs visible at runtime, not polish issues.

1. ~~`selection.css` + `MarqueeLayer.tsx`: `var(--accent)` → `var(--io-accent)`, `rgba(80,180,255,0.08)` → `var(--io-accent-subtle)`. (2-file change.)~~ **Fixed 2026-05-27.**
2. ~~`OpcSources StatusBadge` hex-alpha concat bug: `${color}20` → `color-mix(...)` or component migration.~~ **Fixed 2026-05-27.** `background: \`${color}20\`` → `color-mix(in srgb, ${color} 12%, transparent)`; `border: \`1px solid ${color}40\`` → `color-mix(in srgb, ${color} 25%, transparent)`. Fix scoped to the local `StatusBadge` function in `OpcSources.tsx` (lines 168–170); matches the `color-mix` pattern already used by `SystemHealth.tsx`. **Note:** The same `${color}20`/`${color}40` hex-alpha pattern also appears in `Users.tsx` (Badge, line 108/110), `Roles.tsx` (Badge, line 51/53), `CameraStreams.tsx` (line 785/787), and `MaintenanceTicketsPanel.tsx` (line 52) — these were not fixed here and remain broken when CSS variable strings are passed as the color argument.
3. `alarmFlash.css`: migrate hex to `--io-alarm-*` tokens. (This affects theme support, not current dark-theme functionality, but the hardcoded values are wrong in principle and block HPHMI work.)
4. Critical ARIA fixes: `RestorePreviewModal` + Console inline modals + Import/OpcSources dialogs. (Accessibility regression on destructive paths.)
5. Replace 8 `window.confirm()` calls with `ConfirmDialog`.
6. Console duplicate Publish button — remove one render block.

**Why second:** These are observable regressions. They are small-scope, can be reviewed independently, and should not wait for the broader convergence work.

### Phase 3 — Shared style constants (enables consistent convergence without component work)

Depends on: Phase 1 token fixes (so constants reference valid tokens).

1. Create `shared/styles/buttons.ts` with corrected `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` variants.
2. Create `shared/styles/inputs.ts` with standard `inputStyle` (including focus ring; remove `outline: none`).
3. Migrate Console button and input inline styles to shared constants.
4. Migrate Designer text-action buttons and import buttons to `buttons.ts`; migrate `DesignerRightPanel inputStyle` and `PointPickerModal` to `inputs.ts`.
5. Migrate Settings `Import.tsx`, `BulkUpdate.tsx`, `Sessions.tsx` diverging buttons and inputs to shared constants.
6. Fix `accentColor` / font-family regressions (OpcSources `<style>` tag, monospace font references) as part of input migration.

**Unblocks:** Phase 4 modal work (button styles inside dialogs) and Cat 6/7 cleanup across all modules.

### Phase 4 — Shared components (highest coordination cost)

Depends on: Phase 3 constants (dialogs need buttons; StatusBadge needs token pairs from Phase 1).

1. Promote `FieldLabel` to `shared/components/FieldLabel.tsx`. Migrate usage in Console and Settings.
2. Build `shared/components/StatusBadge.tsx`. Migrate all five Settings implementations, Console priority/state/quality badges, Designer badge indicators.
3. Build `shared/components/Dialog.tsx` (thin wrapper: ARIA, backdrop token, z-index, standard container styles). Migrate Console inline modals and Designer non-Radix dialogs to use it.
4. Fix Designer TEST MODE `<style>` tag injection → CSS keyframe class.
5. Extend `SettingsPageLayout` accessibility or create a simpler page-title shared component for Console and Designer views.

### Phase 5 — Polish and alignment (deferred; no functional impact)

Depends on: Phases 1–4 being stable.

1. Typography: standardize on documented size/weight/spacing values across section labels, field labels, tab labels, page titles.
2. Toolbar heights: define `--io-toolbar-height` token; update Designer skeleton placeholder.
3. Hover/focus: replace remaining DOM-mutation hover with CSS `:hover` pseudo-classes or React state.
4. DesignerCanvas grid line colors, canvas border, guide line colors: token-ify or formally document as intentional.
5. Semantic headings: add `<h2>`/`<h3>` where currently bare `<div>`.
6. Section label `letterSpacing` standardization to 0.06em.

---

## Section 4 — Risks and Unknowns

### R1 — `--io-z-modal: 300` is unusable as-is

Every dialog in all three modules uses z-index values between 1000 and 9999. Raising the token will fix any code that correctly references it, but code currently using hardcoded integers (the majority) will be unaffected until individually migrated. There is a risk that the migration is incomplete and layers will stack incorrectly in edge cases (e.g., a console modal rendered inside a designer tab). A z-index audit across all `zIndex` values in the frontend would be needed to produce a safe z-index scale before setting definitive token values.

### R2 — Light theme and HPHMI theme are not yet implemented; audit was dark-theme-only

The audit verified token references but could not test whether visual output is correct in non-dark themes. `alarmFlash.css` is confirmed dark-theme-only (colors are hardcoded hex matching dark-theme token values). `operationalState.css` is intentionally non-adaptive. Any module-local hardcoded hex values will also fail to adapt. If theme switching is a near-term requirement, the Phase 2 alarmFlash fix and Phase 1 token additions should be front-loaded.

### R3 — DesignerCanvas.tsx at 12,067 lines is high-risk to touch

Multiple Phase 4 items (Dialog promotion, token fixes) require changes inside or adjacent to DesignerCanvas. Any change here should be minimally invasive — prefer aliasing tokens (Phase 1) over in-file search-and-replace of hardcoded values, since the token alias approach reduces the number of touch points in this file.

### R4 — `window.confirm()` replacements require confirming render context

Eight `window.confirm()` calls need to become `ConfirmDialog` renders. Some are inside contexts (OpcSources, Import) that render large tables or multi-step wizard flows. Each replacement needs to verify that the `ConfirmDialog` can be portal-rendered correctly from the calling component's position in the tree, since some callers may be inside transformed ancestors (CLAUDE.md invariant: `position: fixed` inside react-grid-layout transforms breaks — use `createPortal`).

### R5 — BulkUpdate.tsx is a parallel button system inside Settings

`BulkUpdate.tsx` defines its own full button style set (`BTN_PRIMARY`, `BTN_SECONDARY`, `BTN_DANGER`) independent of `settingsStyles.ts`. Before cross-module button standardization is clean, this file needs to be migrated to the shared constants. If it is overlooked, Settings will still have two competing button systems internally, undermining the shared approach.

### R6 — Audit scope was limited to Console, Designer, and Settings

Other frontend modules (OPC config detail views, camera streams, supplemental connectors, display element config panels, SAML/auth provider pages) were not audited. The undefined token references (`--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, `--io-accent-rgb`) and `window.confirm()` calls found in Settings suggest that scope extensions would find additional deviations. Phase 1 token fixes will automatically resolve references to newly-defined tokens across all modules; component migrations would require a separate audit pass.

### R7 — Focus ring removal is a wide-scope accessibility regression

Every interactive element in all three modules suppresses the native focus ring via `outline: none` with no CSS replacement. Fixing this requires touching every input, button, and interactive div — hundreds of instances. The shared constants files (Phase 3) provide the leverage point to fix this in one place for buttons and inputs, but custom interactive elements (palette tiles, canvas nodes, tab strips, status bar segments) need individual fixes. A full accessibility pass scoped to just focus states may be warranted as a standalone workstream.

### R8 — Audit detail limitations in specific areas

The audit did not include source-level review of:
- `CategoryShapeWizard.tsx`, `ShapeDropDialog.tsx`, `RecognitionWizard.tsx`, `IographicImportWizard.tsx` (Designer) — only top-level structural facts were captured; step indicator patterns were noted as inconsistent but not enumerated
- Camera streams and supplemental connectors (Settings) — flagged for `window.confirm()` calls only
- Report scheduling, SMS providers, EULA admin (Settings) — referenced in the reconciliation log as using `--io-modal-backdrop` correctly; not fully audited

These gaps mean the "consistent deviations" counts may understate actual scope.

## Section 5 — Implications for Future Work

### 5.1 Implications for the Claim C Revisit (Workstream 5)

Per `05-claim-c-deferral.md` Section 5, Claim C reopens only after Claim A and Claim B are both complete and reviewed. The following Claim A items have direct downstream effects on Claim C scope:

- **A11 and A12 are consumed by Claim C.** `--io-alarm-inactive` enables `alarmFlash.css` hex migration (05 Section 3.1). `--io-text-inverse` enables the DesignerCanvas resize handle fix (05 Section 3.4). Defining them now means Claim C can reference them immediately without re-entering the token layer.

- **A6 (`--io-error` alias) and A1 (`--io-bg` alias) resolve Claim C imperfections automatically.** Once the aliases exist in `index.css`, DesignerCanvas's context menu destructive color (05 Section 3.5) and WorkspaceGrid's container background (05 Section 3.2) start rendering correctly without any code change in those Claim C files. This reduces the minimum touch-point count inside the high-risk 12,067-line `DesignerCanvas.tsx`.

- **A13 (z-index scale) is a prerequisite for Claim C z-index work.** DesignerCanvas uses internal `zIndex` values in the 300–2000 range. When Claim C touches DesignerCanvas, those values must be coordinated with the scale established in A13. The scale decision is a dependency, not a blocker — Claim C should not begin its z-index work until A13 is decided and committed.

- **A14 (sidebar width) does not directly affect the canvas seam.** The canvas containers (WorkspaceGrid, DesignerCanvas) span the remaining viewport width after the sidebar. The canvas-to-sidebar boundary is clean regardless of which value (220px or 240px) is chosen, as long as the token and code are consistent.

### 5.2 Conventions Established for the Eight-Module Rebuild

The eight modules being rebuilt around the converged Console/Designer foundation will inherit the shell conventions locked in by Claim A. The following must be treated as non-negotiable constraints for all rebuilt modules:

| Convention | Value | Applies to |
|---|---|---|
| Side panel background | `var(--io-surface-secondary)` | Any rebuilt module with a left or right panel. Designer's drift to `var(--io-surface)` is being corrected in B1 to establish this as the unambiguous standard. |
| Active nav item indicator | `borderLeft: 2px solid var(--io-accent)` + adjusted padding | Any rebuilt module with a sidebar nav list. |
| Sidebar width | `var(--io-sidebar-width)` (value per A14 decision) | All side panels in rebuilt modules must use this token, not a hardcoded integer. The value may not be known until A14 is decided — this token must be resolved before any rebuilt module's panel layout is coded. |
| Nav group header typography | 11px / 600 / uppercase / 0.06em / `var(--io-text-muted)` | All section-group labels within sidebar nav panels. |
| Toolbar surface | `var(--io-surface)` + `borderBottom: 1px solid var(--io-border)` | Module toolbars. This is the Console+Designer convention adopted in `04-recommendations.md` Cat 3 — not `var(--io-surface-primary)` (which is the AppShell reference but is not the adopted target for module toolbars). |
| Token hygiene | No token reference may be used that is not defined in `index.css` | Treat any undefined token reference in a rebuilt module as a blocking defect. The token list cleared by Claim A is the authoritative registry; nothing outside it may be referenced. |

**One Claim A item must be resolved before any of the eight modules begins panel-layout work:** A14 (`--io-sidebar-width` value). Building any panel at a hardcoded width and later discovering the chosen value was different requires an eleven-module edit. Decide it once, enforce it everywhere.
